---
title: "R Package"
---

The `statsdata` R package gives you direct access to the StatsData Hub
collection from within R — search for datasets by teaching criteria and
load them straight into your analysis without leaving RStudio.

## Installation

The package is currently available from GitHub. To install it, you first
need the `devtools` package:

```r
install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("emilysomerset/statsdata-hub/statsdata")
```

## Basic usage

### Search for datasets

Find datasets by statistical method, teaching concept, course level,
or discipline:

```r
library(statsdata)

# Browse all available datasets
find_datasets()

# Find datasets suitable for teaching logistic regression
find_datasets(method = "logistic_regression")

# Find datasets for a graduate course in psychology
find_datasets(course_level = "graduate", discipline = "psychology")
```

### Search by specific criteria

```r
# Search by statistical method
search_by_method("multilevel_modeling")

# Search by teaching concept
search_by_concept("confounding")

# Search by variable type
search_by_variable("count")
```

### Load a dataset

```r
# Load a dataset by its ID
titanic <- load_dataset("titanic-survival")
head(titanic)

# Load and start analysing immediately
library(statsdata)
busy <- load_dataset("busy-wellbeing")
```

### Get information about a dataset

Each dataset has a full metadata entry — you can see the available
datasets and their IDs using `find_datasets()`, then load the one
you want with `load_dataset()`.

## Available datasets

See the [Browse Datasets](browse.qmd) page for the full filterable collection.
